sentences.text: The scene is set in hospital. A doctor talks to a nurse at a nurse's station. A patient fills out a form. The nurse tells the doctor that two detectives came buy earlier to see him. They recommend he comes down to the station for questioning. The doctor is nervous. Has he been finally caught after all these years?
Connie gently puts down a tape recorder at her desk. She knew she had him based on evidence, but wanted to get a taped confession. The doctor stands next to her without saying a word.

The doctor asks Connie, "what is this all about?" Connie calmly says, "Sit down. We have some questions for you." John feels sick in the doctor's presence. There is an ominous red glow coming from a street sign outside. The station is otherwise dimly lit.
Connie takes off her coat and begins the interrogation. John pinches his nose. He wants to vomit. This is one of the sickest bastards he has ever encountered.

Connie says, "We know what you did to those kids. We have you dead to rights. Confess and maybe the DA will take the death penalty off the table." The doctor smirks. Connie loses control of her emotions and pushes the doctor backwards and he tumbles from his chair. John sits motionless. He will say he saw nothing.

Shea sat in front of a large, dark brown shelf filled with small knick knacks. His whitish gray hair was swept to the sides of his face and his mouth held his cigarette quite confidently. Smugness oozed off of him as he glanced to the side.

Christian was dressed in a medium blue suit with a darker blue vest. The suite was still somewhat crisp, but you coud tell it had been a long day for Christian. Shea watched as his companion sat down.

Shea removed the cigarette from his mouth, careful not to get any ashes on his white suit. His red and white striped shirt made itself know showing at his wrists and neck. Shea looked at his friend exectantly waving his free hand in the air.

Christian took in Shea's look and he raised his hand to let Shea know that things had not gone as well as they had expected. All the books that they had researched were not helpful and they deserved to be stacked behind him in that corner.
But then Chrisian thought of a brillian idea. He looked at Shea with renewed hope and determination in his eyes. His amber shades emphasizing his confidence. His beard framed a confident look.
It was so amazing, why hadn't they thought of it before. Christian turned his head and held his hand up to his cheek to show that he was pretty sure that their problem could be solved.
The door was slammed open by a woman who entered the room followed by Bradley and another female.
Shea held up his hand asking them to wait a moment so he could hear Christian's great idea.

Christian smiled as he stood up and rushed out of the room. Bradley had saved him again. He didn't have a good idea, not one that Chrisitan would accept, it was time to make his escape before Christian figured that out.
Denzel follows closely behind an older looking gentleman at night
Denzel confronts the older gentleman to ask if he's seen his friends. One girl and two men.
The old man turns and says ha has indeed and provides denzel with their whereabouts
Mila is sitting listening patiently to to tom and gary, who are bickering about what they want to eat for dinner
Tom and gary are sitting in the same room as Mila having a discussion about who's going to be the next one to make dinner
James is speaking on the phone, talking to the police because he believes he saw a murder out his window
James is looking out his window. We can see into the house behind his. The window is clearly visible to him.
James cradles the mouthpiece of his phone. He's worried and listening intently. He hears something outside.
James can hear someone outside his door. He's in danger.
James hears someone trying to open his door.
James had a broken leg that limits jis mobility. He tried desperately to wheel his wheelchair to safety.
James tried and fails to stand up.
George stands over James in shadow, ready to strike.
The door closes, leaving James's fate uncertain.

Jennifer cautiously approached Gary as he sat behind a large desk. She knew how dangerous and capricious he can be. On the desk the ledger sat, opened to the transactions in question. Large candles illuminated the room.

Gary has seen better days. A turf war with a new organization has left him on edge. His empire is weakening. An extension of himself, he feels his own vitality waning. He says, "Can you explain why we've been losing money in our southern district?"

Jennifer knew he would ask about the lost business. She had come prepared to answer his questions, perhaps allow him to understand the new reality of the situation, or shoot him in the heart if necessary. She was a bit shocked at how he looked. She said, "Are you not well?"

Jennifer stood still, one hand touching the table, as Gary looked down without speaking. He had a way of disarming people, simultaneously putting the fear in them while earning their respect, trust, and sometimes their love. He was a brutal man who gave nor asked for quarter.
Gary looked now at the ledger. He still did not speak. Jennifer refused to break the silence. She waited and watched.

Finally Gary looked up, more lines on his face than when Jennifer had seen him a week ago. He stared at her for what seemed an eternity. He saw the qualities he identified when she was ten. Still there, burning brighter than ever. He knew she was ready for what came next. He said, "I think it is time we discuss my succession plan."

Theater is a cut throat experience. Everyone wants your role, your spot on the stage. You rely on your instructor for every bit of guidance they can offer, to keep your spot. Natalie has no idea what is expected of her at this point.
She looks for more instruction, and gets only vague responses. She insists on more detail, exacting, specific to the minor movements. She begs, please, then simply demands.
The instructor at his wits end refuses to give anymore. Minutes go by without a word. He just stares through the floor, like it isn't even there.
Then suddenly, his head pops up and he says the one thing Natalie never wanted to hear. After the initial shock, she storms off to the dressing room.
Ksenia, your up. You are now assuming my role, Natalie explains.
A man is glancing down at his agenda
his wife slowly begins to wake up
as she sits up in bed, the man notices her in the mirror
the man turns around and walks over to the bed. He takes one look at his wifes gorgeus naked body and is suddenly overcome with absolute happiness. He feels like the luckiest man in the world.
The man continues to get ready for work while simultaniously conversing with his wife about the previous nights affairs. He puts his blazer on and says goodbye.
An entrance sign for a diner makes sure people know where to go by including an arrow, as well as text telling people to use the front door.
Betty and Rita follow the instructions and head toward the diner.

Rita stops and hesitates going in. She has been depressed lately, and she really doesn't want to watch Betty try to cheer her up. She also doesn't want to deal with a crowd of people, not to mention the fact that she hasn't had much of an appetite lately and the greasy diner food isn't likely to change that.

Rita tells Betty this was a well-intentioned bad idea, but she'd rather go back home. Betty reminds Rita that she has been just sitting in her house for weeks, which isn't helping the depression. This is the first day in quite a while that Rita has made herself presentable and gone out in public, and Betty can see this has already brightened up Rita's mood a little. Rita still hesitates, but Betty promises her all they will do is sit, and sitting at the diner or sitting at home amounts to the same thing, and they are already here, so why not just go in.

Since Rita clearly doesn't want to talk about her depression or problems she's having, Betty picks up a newspaper and skims it, letting Rita know that if Rita wants to talk, she's available, but if not, that's fine too. Rita relaxes and gets lost in thought.

Tim was intrigued about the fact that his ex girlfriend called him to meet up in a hotel. He wanted to see her again. However he was nervous about the fact that she was a secret witch. He slowly approached her hotel room.
The door was unlocked, which was odd for a hotel. He was wondering if she started up the witchcraft already after promising him she would never use it on him again.
He heard her singing in the shower. She had a beautiful voice. He slowly walked towards where the shower was and eventually saw her in the bathroom waiting for him.
He gave her a warm embrace. He missed her voluptuous body. He was wondering if everything was going to be ok after all.

Suddenly, she turned into a hideous monster. He finally realized that this was all just an act. She wanted to harm him. He beautiful skin melted away to reveal withered, spotty skin. She let out a huge growl.
Tim wouldn't let her monstrous form intimidate him. He explained that their relationship was over and that he was going to leave for good.

The continuous, circular wall of the inside of the command center is covered in 4 inch bubble lights, emitting a dull yellow glow. Rosario, the main researcher, stands on a landing platform, looking down at her worker Anthony. The lights reflect off her brown leather jacket and frame her in an eerie light. Rosario calls down to Anthony to begin the energy transfer.

Down below, Anthony looks at his transparent display providing information about the fusion reactor and its components. Seeing that all components meet safety checks, Anthony calls out that he's moving to engage the fusion reactor.
Anthony looks down from his console and reaches down below to access the cabinet holding the fusion reactor's ignition switch, a cylinder tube with grip handles.
Anthony grabs onto the reactor's fusion switch and pushes it into the console, which engages the fusion reactor.

The dim bubble lights surrounding the inside of the command center all begin to slowly pulse, confirming the fusion reactor has begun powering up. The lights look like the eyes of a fly, witnessing this major breakthrough via a million reflections.
George lay awake in bed. His head was on the soft pillow but he could not close his eyes and go to sleep. Thoughts raced through his head as time ticked away into the night.
Jacques had already given George an ultimatum at work on the prior day. Now he was about to give his other employees a stern warning to improve their efficiency.
Cary and Audrey were afraid their jobs might be in jeopardy like George's. They listened intently to the harsh words of Jacques as he yelled loudly at them
Cary didn't seem to be as worried as Audrey. Cary had always disliked Jacques and after he finished yelling at them he was going to express his dislike for Jacques.
Ned sat quietly next to Audrey and Cary. He always thought he was a hard worker but after George was given an ultimatum by Jacques he thought he too could be fired or replaced.
The small furry brown dog looks up from its position, lying on a hardwood floor, surrounded by a few scraps of debris. The dog feigns a look of innocence, but it is obviously guilty of some offense.
Ben, dressed in black shirt and blue jeans, stands above the dog, looking both angry and aghast.
The dog reacts by leaping and snapping at Ben's hand, attaching itself with its teeth. Ben responds with a scream and just manages to shake the dog loose.
Ben decides to stand his ground, crouching and taunting the dog to "Come and get another piece of me, you mongrel!"
Ben stands in the middle of the living room, facing his would-be challenger. The dog appears unfazed and prepared to do battle, lifting a paw as if ready to charge.

The dog leaps, lunging for Ben's vulnerable crotch. Ben doubles over in pain, shrieking. The scene catches the attention of Cameron, who holds her hands out in shock and confusion. She screams at Ben and the dog to stop fighting.
A man outside watches as a scene unfolds, unsure of what to do.
A man with a large gun gains entry into the bar. Angry, he holds people hostage while he robs the place.
The man sees someone who is not doing as he says, he holds up his gun to shoot.
The gun becomes jammed, giving everyone a chance to run and escape.
With the people freed, the man from outside raises his gun and takes aim toward the man that is inside the building.
Jeff and Jim have crashed a wedding. They're wearing obnoxious suits and are banging down drinks at the bar.
Karen and Charles are naturally upset about this. These two men are disrupting their party. They're incredulous.
Jim and Jeff are not phased by the complaints. They plan to do something even more outrageous. They don't need to speak, they know each other too well.
Jim and Jeff lean in to kiss each other, as a joke.
After the kiss, Jim leans back against the bar. He's floored by the experience.
The sound of keystrokes echoed throughout the room. Tom was posting to a message board hoping to land a much-needed job. He thought that he could stand out by starting his resume with a poem.

Tom pulled out some paperwork from his desk. He needed to list some references after the poem but couldn't remember the phone number for his last employer. Just then his roommate Jean walked in holding a pint glass.

Tom jumped, not thinking Jean would be home anytime soon. He was hoping to finish his resume and have it sent off before Jean returned. Jean was loud and mentioned wanting to get a drink from the neighborhood pub. Tom thought about it, but realized being unemployed meant not having extra money to day drink.
Tom was still thinking about that poem he was working on for his resume. He started reciting it out loud, not even thinking about Jean being in the room.
Jean turned to Tom thinking he was crazy. But then he remembered he had been drinking. Maybe this was all in his mind. He decided that maybe day drinking wasn't such a good idea after all.
Dane, sporting a couple week's scruffy beard growth, gritted his teeth as he pulled the car up next to the high-rise building.
As someone approached his car, Dane looked out the driver's window to find out what they wanted.

It was Demi, the last person he wanted to see. At her request, Dane exited his car. Demi's partner Reuben, wearing a fedora at a jaunty angle atop his head, stood in the background, leaning against another car.

Demi, stylish in black attire, confronted Dane, accusing him of botching a job. Meanwhile, Reuben remained in the back with his thumbs tucked in the waistband of his pants and his expression concealed behind aviator sunglasses.
In the face of Demi's not inaccurate appraisal, Dane looked sheepish. He tried to explain himself, but Demi was not having it.

Two goofy-looking guys, played by Jeff and Jim, are sitting on a big bed in a really fancy room. They’re both wearing fluffy white bathrobes. One of them has messy blond hair and looks confused, while the other is rubbing his eye with a sleepy face and has a sleep mask on his forehead. The bed appears to be covered in a lot of money! It’s all over the place, like they were using it as blankets. There’s a tray with tea or breakfast next to them.

The same two goofy-looking guys, played by Jeff and Jim, are now sitting close together and staring at a newspaper. They’re still wearing their fluffy white bathrobes, and one of them still has the sleep mask pushed up on his head. They both have really serious looks on their faces, like they’re trying hard to understand something important. The guy with messy blond hair is even making a funny face, like he’s confused or surprised. Their expressions make it look like they’re reading something shocking or strange.

Now we see that the two men are reading a newspaper with a big title. It says that the Swanson family is having a big party tomorrow night. Many important people from the city will come to the party. There is a picture of three people in the paper. The paper says their names are Karla, Mary, and Karl Swanson. The two men are looking closely at the newspaper while wearing their bathrobes. They seem very interested in what the paper says about the party.

Now the two guys are getting haircuts. They both have big silver bowls on their heads to shape the hair. Jim is sitting with an older man cutting his hair carefully. Jeff is having his hair cut by a younger woman. They still look a little excited but maybe a bit nervous too. They want their hair to look good, probably for the big party they read about. The room looks plain, like a simple barbershop, but the silver bowls make the haircuts look funny. They are getting ready and trying to look nice.

Now the haircuts are finished. The two guys are sitting under big, old-fashioned hooded dryers. Two ladies are lifting the dryers up to show their new hairstyles. The guys look at their hair with surprise and curiosity. The dryers are loud and big, and the room looks like a typical hair salon. The guys are probably wondering if their new hair looks good enough for the fancy party. This moment shows them getting ready and seeing how they look after the haircut.
Val, wearing a ski mask and a suit stands on a balcony overlooking an open office in a city building.

Robert and two coworkers, a man and a woman, sit in their chairs. Robert and the male coworker have their hands clasped behind their heads. The woman has her hands up against her face, in fear. Robert looks grim.
Val holds a machine gun and looks at other workers crouched down.
Val is looking into a bank vault, the door open.
Val stands against the wall, his rifle pointed to the front of the office.
She was blind. Blind since birth, in fact. But she knew the world around her through sound and touch. Touch like the soft cotton of a sheet hanging on a clothesline, drying in the wind.

She also knew the sound of chopping wood, for she'd heard it nearly every day for her twenty-five years. She could even tell who was doing the chopping based upon their strength and rhythm. In this case, it was Willem.

She hadn't talked to him Willem since the night he tried to put her hand on his warm spot without asking. It was wrong for her brother to do that and he knew it. She screamed, or at least she thought she did, and ran off into the fields.
Willem felt terrible about this but he didn't see how they could fix what happened. The damage had been done. He kept his distance.
He watched as she sprinted from the house to the well to get water. There was no clear reason for her rush. It could only be that she did not want to be caught by Willem again. His heart sank.
He could see her there, stooped over. He wanted to go to her but resisted.
It was time for him to leave his home. He knew it. She knew it. The wind knew it.
Would he even tell her?
A girl lays her head on her older sister's lap as they sit on the stairs. Her older sister strokes her hair to comfort her.
A man wearing a blood drenched shirt approaches them.
Her older sister gets off the stairs to confront the man.
The man crouches down in pain.
He is bleeding heavily, and his hand drips with his blood.
The man frowns at his hand.
Another man looks on as the bloodied man collapses.
The man has died and is buried in the backyard. The girl, her older sister and a few others stand over his grave, mourning him.
The big game is here. Two rival football teams take the field. The kicker approaches the ball and is about to smash it down the field. The crowd anticipates an exciting game.
The quarterback and coaches of the blue team look on. They expect an easy win over the M.A.S.H. team. The coach in military uniform says, "These guys are sissies."

Rene and Roger stand watching their team. Rene quietly mutters a prayer and then smiles. Rene is confident because he got himself a ringer. Roger is aware but doesn't realize just how good the recruit is. "Watch this" says Rene as their secret weapon receives ball. The military men in the stands place their bets.

Number 44 is the M.A.S.H team's star. He reluctantly agreed to play this exhibition game. He played college ball and was pretty good, but he had put the game behind him. He receives the ball from the kickoff and races through the blue team. None of them expected his speed and his evasiveness.
He scores a touchdown with ease. M.A.S.H is winning out of the gate and the players of the blue team realize that this game is going to be much more difficult than they anticipated.

The credits for a movie are shown all shot in black and white. A man poses for a photograph dressed in a suit with a bowtie. He is standing next to two women dressed in dresses with gloves and fur. The photo looks dated. The photograph accompanies a title card for the producer of the movie.

Five men pose for a photograph. The men are all dressed nicely in suits. Their poses are more casual than formal. It's as if they were attending a party and got together for a photo. The photograph accompanies a title card for the film editor of the movie.

A couple converse together while four women pose behind them in a photograph. In another photo beside it, a man conducts a band. Everyone is dressed nicely as if they were attending a party. The four women wear hats and dresses. The conductor uses a baton to conduct the band and is wearing glasses.

A group of people stand and pose along a building's wall outside for a photo. There are nine people in the photo from various ages. Some are dressed in fancier clothes than the others. Their attention is on two children playing in front of them. The title card for the actor Colin Farrell is beside the photo.

A man poses for a photograph while showing off some drawings pinned to a wall. There are various drawings on the wall other than the one he is pointing at. He has a smile on his face and is dressed in casual work clothes. The title card for the actor Bradley Whitford is beside the photo.
A woman in a pink bathrobe is sitting in a darkened room. A coffee table is in front of her. She appears to be despondent.
In a close up it is seen that she is an attractive, blonde woman, maybe in her 30s and seems to be troubled.
There is a closer look focusing on her right eye and the area around it. The eye is blue and denotes sadness.
We see a wider view of the woman a little above the waist up and she appears terrified, probably screaming, her mouth wide open.
Now she appears to be in a different room with smoke filling it. She is lying on a bed, possibly resting, injured, or even dead.
Brian just received some disturbing news and he is very concerned about what could happen. He knows this is a very serious situation.
Joan is on the phone with a someone and is getting an update on the situation, while Brian watches her.
Joan flashes Brian a look, as if to say, this situation is more dire than we could have imagined.
Julia is watching the situation unfold and she is also very concerned, because she understand the potential consequences of what is happening.
Matt is on the call with Joan, as he aims his gun at the office that Brian and Joan are in. He has demands that he wants met.
Tom is talking to a staff member, who is on the computer trying to pinpoint Matt's location.
Brian and Julia discuss the demands being made by Matt, while Joan continues to talk to Matt on the phone.
Julia is distraught about the situation and is weighing the options.
Joan is coming to terms with the enormity of the situation and realizes that there is very little she can do to change it. She knows they cannot realistically meet Matt's demands.
amy pulled up to the house in her powder blue caddy. christian had called her and asked if she could come take him to the hospital.
he stood out front clutching his chest.
amy rushed out of the car to get christian.
she reached his side just as he appeared to be falling out. she caught him just before he fell.

she helped him to the car and he had to get in through the driver side because the passenger door doesn't open. her plan was to drive him to the hospital and then notify his brothers bradley, alessandro, and louis.
James and Kim enjoyed fine dining at a ritzy hotel with a full course dinner prepared by a fine chef.
Kim caught the eye of James from across the room and he couldn't help but stare at her from a distance.
James admired Kim's green dress and her beautiful blonde hair. He wished he was sitting at the table with her instead of the other guy.
When she left he thought he might follow her to see where she might go.
James got behind the wheel of his white sedan to follow Kim.
He waited to see where she went and tried to be inconspicuous by pretending to read his paper intently as he sat in his car outside her hotel.

In a diner, three funeral home directors discuss the business of death over coffee. Todd, who sits in the center, is describing the rising costs he has witnessed in coffin manufacturing. To his right sits Benjamin, a rather dour man, and to his left sits Crawford, a legend in the industry. Todd asks, "How do we keep our costs down in this economy?" Crawford is silent for a moment and says, "I don't think we can without reducing the quality of our services."

Todd pays for Benjamin and Crawford. He owes them everything. They showed him how to run a mortuary; how to simultaneously be solemn and gentle with families of the deceased while milking them of every penny he can. He saw nothing wrong with it. He has paid his dues at this point, but still feels some loyalty to his mentors. "Thank you, Loretta. I hope you have a good day."

Benjamin and Todd walk out of the diner. Benjamin says, "Look, you need to cool it with Crawford. You are making it too obvious you want to take over his funeral homes when he retires in the next couple of years." Todd says, "Yeah, and so do you."

Todd looks at Benjamin as he again talks about how they should split Crawford's business evenly. "We can control this entire market together, says Benjamin. There is little traffic on the highway. Todd is noncommittal and says,"Sure, maybe we could."

Todd stares at Benjamin as he looks down in discomfort. He is leery of Todd. Todd is a shark. His ambition was dangerous. There was plenty of death to go around. It was lucrative. Why couldn't Todd see it doesn't have to be winner takes all? Benjamin then says, "Hey just think about it. Crawford has no family left besides us."

Resting his hand on the edge of the church pew he waits in anticipation for what the preacher is going to say. The thoughts of the last passing days clearly evident in the uneasiness of his hands position and the motion of his fingers, the tensness of his wrist seen at his black pettie coats edge.

Not alone in his unease, the church seems filled with individuals unsure of their own salvation. One looks downcast at the floor, head cocked to the left and eyes closed, it tensed contemplation, as if he is asleep, his dark gray pettiecoat unbuttoned at the top, reaving a light gray dress shirt. Just infront of him is a women, he face obscured, but her dark brown overcoat and pink stipped dress looking disheveled, as if she has hurried her way to forgiveness. Her neck tense, matching the tightness of her drawstring on her bonnet. Directly to her side and behind her is a women with dark blonde hair, her eyes closed in deep prayer.

The preacher, seemingly unaware of his congregation's plight, stands at a tall dark wooden podium and begins to prostilicide out of a large bible, his eyes focused upin it, instead of his congregation. The large arches of the church ceiling behind him, allowing his voice to echo throgh the halls of eternity. Before him, four members off the church sit quietly, there eyes fixated onto him. The women on the left has a leather bonnet wrapped in a gray cloth tied into a bow, with the women to her right wearing a traditional dark dray bonnet, both having a stillness like statues.

As the preacher pauses her serman for moment, the wearyness and tensness can be seen upoon his face. His limps perced and his tension lines visable around his eyes and his mouth. The weight of the town resting upon his shoulders and the torn and worn pages of a strongly used bible showing the gravity of the situation. The seperation off members of the church seen behind him to the right, just in view.

This seperation draws attention and two members of the congregation begin to talk amonst themselves, as a women looks on behind them worryingly, her dark drown dress drapped upon her body and her worried eyes and frowning face evident for everyone to see. Just behind her the man in the black pettie coat and white dress shirt sits. So distracted and tortured by his thoughts that he is seemingly unaware of what is going on around him. The women behind him to his right looking on in shock at the congrgation whule the one on his left, much older than the first and wearing a fanned black hat, looks on with an angry weary face, her eyes fixated forward on those in front of her in the church pew that choose to gossip. Echoing the complexities of small town colonial life.

The witching hour had hit at GroupLens. It was 3 PM and, like clockwork, Nick and Dexter had arrived at Jason's place. What would the conversation for today be? One thing you do know is that when 3 PM hits the lab the productivity hits the fan. Jason was nervous today though. He had been waiting to start his journey for the past 3 months and finally the day had come. It was the final day of classes before the December Holiday break. All his finals were done and assignments were in. Jason had only one more semester of college left. He knew that if he was ever going to graduate then he had to leave now in order to be back in time for the Spring 2026 semester. Little did he know what lay ahead of him in a trip that would change his life in ways he never imagined.

Nick had never climbed a mountain. He had conquered a few mountains, figuratively, in his life, but never had he seen the world from over 14,000 feet. While he had been on top numerous times in his life (if you know what I mean), this would be the ultimate experience. As Nick prepared to leave, a tear came to his eye, knowing that he might never make it back alive to the city he loved.

More tears came to his eyes when he remembered he had invited his pesky friend Dexter to come along on his journey. Jason, being driven by the goal at hand, started envisioning their destination: the snowcapped peaks of Mt. Hood. It was no Everest, but when you have never been mountain climbing before, Oregon's highest peak was exotic enough. Jason started to take mental inventory for the journey: parkas, sherpas, St. Bernards with multitudes of firewater... these gentlemen were as ready as they ever could be.

But really, Jason's biggest fear wasn't the climb, the cold, the crevasses that had consumed so many climbers in recent years... it was the journey-long separation from his first love, his true love: technology. With only a GPS and some crappy communication gadgets to appease his needs, this was going to be a very trying journey.

At this point in his musings, Jason became distracted by a leathery scraping sound. It slid through the car rhythmically, like the sound of a young maiden shucking corn. It was, however, the doom-laden soundtrack of red-eyed gremlins in a laborious crawl up Nick's throat and into his lap. Doll-sized bundles of filth and fury, the bilious mal-creatures writhed into acclimation with the bright light of the car. Jason observed this unholy birth with shock-glazed calm.
Onlookers were shocked as to what was going on in the bar.
Jason was not happy and was making sure that everyone knew and felt his anger. He reached out his hand to knock away what was in front of him.
A man was knocked over during this outburst and he was on the ground for a little while.
Jason got some sort of small statue from one of the men who was at the bar. This might have been what all this fuss was over.
The man who was knocked over was now coming to as he opened his eyes from the position he was in.
The anger continued as someone was so mad that they grabbed the tablecloth and ripped is off of the table with everything on it.
This made things fly and eventually some liquid in a pitcher ended up covering Nick.
Two soldiers are being blessed by a chaplain in the middle of a war zone. They are hoping that they are given strength for the battle ahead.
Above them, cattle is being flown in by a helicopter to give the soldiers supplies in the front lines.
As night falls, soldiers try to celebrate and get their minds off of the task that is ahead of them the next day.
A soldier sitting at one of the tables tries to tell a story to the others to help them ease up and laugh a little.

Frederic interrupts him however telling him that he should focus telling scary stories since they're going to have to embrace the horrors that they're about to see the next day. At least horror stories will set them in the right state of mind.
The other soldiers start to listen to Frederic tell his horror story.
But Martin interrupts him and tells everybody the twist of the horror story, spoiling the ending for everybody. Instead, Martin tells the others that they need to drink to get ready for tomorrow.
The others proceed to laugh and drink. Other soldiers take a look at some of the plans for tomorrow. The general takes a look at a piece of paper.

But when Frederic asks if the general is looking at some of the battle plans for tomorrow, the general replies "yes". The general then adds, "And here's a twist for you, you're going to be at the very front of the convoy of tanks."
Ben investigated a noise and was startled to find a small aggressive dog in his girlfriend Cameron's house.
The small dog looked nice at first, but quickly ran after Ben and then jumped up and bit him. Ben needs to defend himself from the attack.
Ben tries desperately to fight off the dog.
Ben rolls around on the floor and wrestles with the tiny dog. He uses ridiculous wrestling moves while Cameron and Lin watch and scream in horror.
Lin is yelling and howling while Ben finally subdues the tiny dog.
Robert and his deputy bust into the house and confront the felon they've been hunting. "Keep your hand where I can see them" says Robert. You need to move nice and slow.
The felon says "You're not going to take me a live" and reaches for his gun. He fires a few shots and jumps to the ground.

Robert and his deputy are forced to jump outside and take cover. They fire off a few shots but they are also not able to get a good look at the felon to finish him off. Robert tells his associates to take cover.
The felon grabs his rifle and takes aim at Robert and his deputy. He has Robert dead in his sights.

Before the felon can pull the trigger, Robert's deputy gets off a shot and hits the felon dead in the head. They walk back into the house and Robert says "nice shooting Deputy." The deputy says, "it didn't have to go down this way."
Phillip was locked away in the booth intently listening in on the conversation that was being tapped. Jude was watching him intently.
Matt came to Jude to find out how they were doing with the conversation and if they got any new information yet.
Matt was not happy to hear that they had not gotten anything new while listening in.
Matt wanted to get to Phillip in the booth to let him know something to listen for.
Matt was very angry and wanted to make sure he was heard and felt by all in this room.
Jude and Phillip now were both listening in as something peeked their interests in the conversation.
This did not amuse Matt very much as they kept him locked out of the booth and only able to watch on.

Matt and Jude are walking together on a wide walkway. There are ornate buildings and about 15 people seen behind them in the background--some people walking, a couple sitting, and others standing together. Jude is carrying a briefcase and Matt is looking at piece of paper in one hand, while another is in his other hand.
Matt now seems to be looking inside the briefcase that Jude was carrying. We see that he is a young man with blonde hair, and wearing glasses.
Now there is a close up of Jude, a handsome young man wearing a black hat.
At this point Matt is looking with some intensity at Jude who seems concerned or even fearful.
Now we see only Jude who is amongst some small motorbikes and looking back at something.
After years of bloodshed and civil war, King Eric now holds the crown. A feast celebrating his coronation is held in the hall.
King Eric made many enemies getting the crown. Just one of many daily precautions he takes is having his food tested for poison.
He looks over his guests, wondering whom to trust and whom to fear.
King Eric isn't just being paranoid. His advisors Mark and David plot his murder, even while they feast on his food.

However, the actual author of the murder plot is David's mother, who has been a bitter but secret enemy of King Eric's family for decades. They ruined her father with a shady land deal and she had to grow up in poverty. No one suspects that the quiet, old woman has thought of nothing but revenge ever since.

Gerard is pedaling down the boardwalk with Jennifer, his current rickshaw passenger. His lackadaisical demeanor is irritating Jennifer, who has plans at the Trump Taj Mahal Casino. Despite this, she reaches into her purse to extract a five dollar bill to tip Gerard with.

As the rickshaw comes to a stop in front of the casino, Gerard places a foot on the ground, waiting. Gerard casually glances around. Tourists meander around the front of the casino; he knows a new fare will be easy to nab.
Jennifer's beautiful blonde hair blows gently in the wind as she prepares to exit the buggy. She hands him the bill, thanks him for the service and exits the yellow and white buggy.
Gerard wishes her well and lightly teases her about her casino plans. She thanks him again, tossing him a wave and a smile.
Gerard pedals down the boardwalk, hoping to find a fare at the other end. The weather is overcast...people appreciate his service more during this type of weather.
Kevin sits starring out the car window debating on what his next move should be.
He reaches over to tell William to hold on and wait to see what is going to happen next. They don't want to jump the gun and start something they shouldn't.
He continues to tell his story about one time he accidently did something he wasn't suppose to and it cost them their mission.
The sun begins to rise with nothing happening as they sit in the car.
They end up turning the car on and leaving without actually accomplishing their goal.
William gets out the car and pounds on the hood angry at the situation.
Various patients with obvious bandaged wounds are in a hospital in the early 1900's. A variety of people walk around the hospital beds.
Jude is on a hospital bed with a clear neck wound that it covered in a blood-soaked bandage. He is in agony as he lays down and closes his eyes.
Nicole searches the hospital beds looking for Jude.
Nicole finally finds Jude and leans over his bed to let him know that she is there.
Nicole has a concerned look in her eyes as she listens to Jude talk.
ellen had a very dysfunctional family.
her problems stemmed from her husband robert being an alcoholic and a drug abuser. all he did lately was lay around on the sofa all day drunk and high.
one day ellen had had enough and had thoughts of going upside his head.
luckily for robert, their son leonardo intervened and discouraged his mom from doing robert harm. ellen vocally expressed to robert that she was fed up.
robert yelled at both leornardo and ellen and seemingly feigned disgust for the both of them.
leonardo verbally abused robert as well and said that he was good for nothing.
robert told leonardo that if he was man enough to speak to him like that then he was man enough to fight. he raised his dukes and invited leornardo to a donnybrook.
this time ellen intervened and did not let the fight take place.
Sam sat on a log in the woods waiting for Richard and Brooke to return so they could resume their hike through the woods.
But Richard and Linda walked slowly enjoying the woods with no idea they were holding Sam up.
But it was no matter. Because Richard made it to his destination. And as soon as he saw the water he took off his shoes and waded into the cool water on a warm day.
He picked a smiling Linda up out of the water and carried her around on his back and his shoulders as she laughed and giggled while the dog ran around at his feet.
And Linda ran around in the water playing with the dog.
And followed behind Richard as he marched through the shallow water in the river on the hot summer day
Angelina is distraught after seeing what was on the screen in front of her: her parents had been captured by an evil criminal organization.
The rest of the room was shocked; they assumed she was joking. Hoever, at further inspection of the screen, they found that she had not been lying.
Still a bit drunk, johnny suggested to go in themselves and take the bad guys out, and matthew excitedly agrees. The drunk men think they can take down this org.
Angelina stops them and explains that this idea will get them nowhere, and probably her father killed.
Fisher, a hacker who was hack into their video feed, was watching them the whole time.
Boss, they know. It's only a matter of time now, Fisher said to his boss.
A boy lies on the grass looking at the clouds. He contemplated what each cloud looked like.
Next to him is a little girl who's doing the same. They live a happy life not knowing What their dad is up to.
A hit was ordered on the little boy's dad, Joe. He was shot two times and was in a serious condition.
The mom showed up to the hospital worried. She asked Joe why he was shot and who could have possibly hated him. To her, her husband Joe was a hard-working husband.
Joe confessed everything to his wife. He regretted being in the business and hoped he could get out of it.
Meanwhile his enemies were in good spirits.
They strolled along happily near the beach as they thought that Joe was dead.

They talked about how glad they were the hit worked and how glad they were that Joe was finally gone. Some of their worries were finally solved. Little did they know that after joe got better, he would start thinking about revenge.

Harrison sprinted through the dense jungle, leaping over the terrain while swatting branches and leaves out of the way. He was told there was nobody living on this part of the river but clearly that was not the case. He had to get out of there fast.

He didn't remember exactly where he left his plane on the river but he didn't have time to take out his map and plot a proper course. He could hear voices yelling behind him, getting louder. They were getting closer. Just when he started to think about having to turn and defend himself, he came up to the riverside and leaped in.

This wasn't exactly safe. There were all sorts of ways to die in that river...crocodiles, piranhas, snakes...heck even drinking it might kill you. But staying out of the water was even more dangerous. He had to swim.
Once he got a little further out he paused to look around and get his bearings.

Looking back from where he came, he could see a group of indigenous people yelling and drawing their bows. They were not firing, however, so it must have been out of their range already. They probably didn't want to waste precious arrowheads on someone they couldn't hit anyway. They'd already gotten their point across.

Realizing he was safe from them, Harrison turned his attention back to finding his plane. It wasn't long before he spotted it, a little downriver from where he expected but that was just fine with him. He needed to get out of there asap.
This is a man you do not mess with. Smiling or not, he is never happy. He wants a reason to end you, and it doesn't need to be a good one.

He can often be found sitting down at a table for a game of card. It often presents the opportunity to proclaim someone is a cheater. Often seen as a good reason to draw a pistol, and no one asks questions.
This is Daniel, an unsuspecting card player. He is looking at a royal flush in his hand. He knows there is absolutely no way that he loses it.
That is until this man goes all-in. Daniel isn't second guessing his hand, just the outcome of winning it.
That's when the question is raised. "Do you think you might want to fold?" and Daniel fully understands, he does in fact, want to fold.
A student, Reese, raises her hand in the middle of a packed classroom.
The teacher, Matthew, standing in front of the class chalkboard, calls on her.
Reese talks to the class about something that was published in the school newsletter.

She directs their attention to the latest issue of Carver High School's newsletter, The Looking Glass. The cover is red and black with a red pawprint on the black portion, Carver, and The Looking Glass printed in black with handles on each of the Os in Looking Glass, making them seem like magnifying glasses.
Inside the newsletter is a black and white photograph featuring a group of smiling students with Reese standing right in the middle of them.
Greta is putting on another one of those Yacht parties that everyone is talking about. They attract some of the normal types of characters. Including Jake.
Jake is your typical ladies man, who is always trying to woo anyone willing to listen. There is a problem though.

Greta has a man already. And not at all in the mood to be "woo'ed" today. So she picks up her phone and auto dials. As the listens to Jake's sales pitch. The same old tired noise, with a hint of creeper to it.
Ashton, Great's boyfriend, arrives on site just as expected. No traffic excuses today.

Just as he is about to move in to make sure the rules of engagement are laid out plainly for Jake, he gets a text. It's Greta, she is calling off the rescue. Seems Jake has gotten the message in the nick of time. Guess Ashton will just have to go find a tv and beer to watch.
Val walks into the professional building and goes into the elevator. Strapped with bullets, a gun, and a mission, Val is going to get what he came after.
Seated in the building are workers in an office. Val walks in and tells everyone to not move. No one knows what to do as Val walks past all of them to the hallway.
Everyone is left scared not knowing what to do or say. Marcus cries at his desk thinking that there is no way that he will see his family again.
Robert feels like there is something he can do, but he watches as helplessly as those around him at his desk as Val walks out of view.
Val walks to the safe, enters it, and starts to take money of if it and put it into his bag.
Just as he is done, he turns toward the door to leave. The door has been closed on him from the outside. Robert had closed the door.
Along with his bags of money and rifle, Robert carries an injured Val away from the shootout after their bank robbery was intercepted by the cops.
But Al continues to purse them despite all the bullets flying around. He also makes sure to get the bystanders out of the way.
Robert looks all around him but sees that the devastation. Cars are left shredded with bullet holes. All the cops who haven't been killed are also descending on him and Val.
But despite the harrowing task of escaping, Robert continues to find a path to get away.
Al and another cop tend to an injured fellow officer on the ground at a grocery store parking lot. The sight only motivates Al to continue to try to catch the bank robbers.
Colin and Jamie have been led into the crime boss's office. Members of the crime gang stand around to protect the boss and intimidate Colin and Jamie.
The crime boss, John, tells Colin and Jamie to sit. Gang enforcers stand in the background.
Colin and Jamie sit down across from crime boss John. One of his goons stares at Colin and Jamie.
Colin and Jamie are now sitting. They're scared but trying to appear relaxed. The boss looks at them while members of his gang are visible in the background.
John addresses Jamie. He tells him that it's been a long time since he last saw him. He says he has a job for him and Colin.
The assistant hands Christoph the dynamite that the assassins were caught with.
During the interrogation, Christoph tries to get Brad, who is masked, to talk.
But Brad refuses to talk making Christoph even angrier.
Christoph threatens that if Brad doesn't talk that it will result in him tying the dynamite to each of the other assassins that they caught and blowing them up to smithereens.

This threat finally gets Brad to talk about their plans to kill Christoph's boss. Christoph is happy to finally get Brad to talk. Unfortunately for Brad, he hears the dynamite detonate a few seconds later.
A young woman reporter is standing outside of a church. She is standing in front of a camera talking about recent murders that occurred at the Church of Manson.
The scene quickly shifts to a picture of Charles Manson who is a well known psychopathic cult leader who runs his own church in Jackson, MS.
The news story goes on to report that bodies were found recently in a ditch near the Church of Manson. Police are suspecting that Manson or his clan has something to do with the murders.

The news story goes on to show the bodies of 4 recently deceased Jackson police. They are lying on beds in a morgue. They were recently killed in a shootout with it is suspected members of the Manson Church.
The police have set up a check point and are on the lookout for the Manson gang and are asking for anyone with information to call the Jackson PD. The Manson clan is said to be armed and dangerous.
A woman stares intently as if lost in her own thoughts
The man is talking up her friend trying to nudge his way into their girls night out
The friend returns with two glasses of champagne and proposes a toast. The woman meets her with a look of disgust. She wanted so badly for the man to have spoken to her instead.
Unable to conceal her emotions, frustrated, she heads inside by herself.
Not willing to throw in the towel just yet, she takes matters into her own hands and puts herself out there. She's not going home alone tonight.
A woman is seen prior to the engagement with the other guests.
The woman has a drink with another party attendee. They are drinking campaign. The two are dressed in elegant dresses for the evening at the party.
The woman then is seated with other party guests at a table. They are also having champaign drinks for the celebration.
The other woman who was outside, is also now inside at a table with another party guest. They are happily sharing a cigarette together, with other party attendees in the background.
The man and woman who were previously sharing the cigarette are now engaging in conversation with the man looking at something and the woman looking towards them in disagreement.
Wolf stands with a group of the crew of the ship, on the deck of the ship.
The crew stand together and cheer on the meeting between the military commander Wolf, and the business man Paul, who is dressed in a nice suit from the 30's.
Paul has a serious discussion with wolf in front of the crew of the ship.
The solders under the command of Wolf point their rifles at Paul, who does not back down.
Wolf physically restrains Karen by holding her arms, while the crew of the boat are surrounded by the military unit who is under the command of Wolf.
Shia is having trouble explaining the situation that he has found himself in, across the table from the law.
Billy reaches into this suit coat pocket looking for a piece of information to show Shia.
After placing the information on the table for Shia to see, Billy addresses the question, 'What about this?' referring to the piece of evidence he has just brought out.
Shia stares on in disbelief, trying to understand how Billy came up with that evidence against him.
Billy is happy with the outcome of his efforts to catch Shia in his questioning.
A couple gathers at a dinner. They know they are going to break up. Both are relatively quiet.
Finally, the woman decides she is going to break the silence. She tells him she will always love him, but things are not going to work out.
The man suddenly reaches into his pocket without saying a word.
A man at the counter who's been watching him wonders if he is going to pull out a gun. He's worried and tenses up.
The woman looks across the table and says "Don't." No one knows what is going to happen next.
Jeff appears to be drunken as he leans lazily to the side on his horse.
Hailee and Matt look on incredulously as Jeff begins to roll of the back of his horse.
Amazingly Jeff lands on his feet after rolling off the back of his horse as he staggers slightly on the ground.
Matt looks on unimpressed despite Jeff's acrobatic dismount from his horse.
Meanwhile Jeff appears agitated and angry as he waves his six shooter in the air and looks around defiantly.

Meanwhile Matt looks at his pistol wondering if he has the same one as Jeff and perhaps thinking about whether or not he could have performed the same acrobatic dismount and come up guns blazing like Jeff.
Emile sits in a chair, bent over a barrel. He is reaching deep into the barrel as if he is searching for something. His hair is disheveled and he's wearing a long sleeved blue shirt.
Emile looks up from the barrel and reaches his hand into a bag on a nearby table. His face gives off the impression that he is frustrated, like he can't find what he is looking for.
Emile lays on his stomach on the floor. He has a book in his hands. His expression is enthralled in the book. It has his complete attention. He looks at it wide eyed.
Emile continues to read the book but now has a pained expression on his face. His is grimacing as he continues reading from the book.
Emile has a shocked expression on his face. He brings a small object closer to his face and looks at it carefully.

Tom, wearing his dark military uniform, thinks through a tough decision. His eye is still healing from the wounds suffered in the war but he will never see in it again. The light is low and the tension is thick.

Tom keeps his eye on the doorway, waiting for his commanding officer to enter the room. His resolve is strong, he knows what he must tell them. In his mind, the war can only move forward one way or all will be lost. If his commanding officer does not enter soon, Tom will go to them. He knows that this decision cannot be put off any longer.

With only one lamp on in the room, Bill's looks over his map to gain a better understanding of where he must move his troops. The enemy has most of their position surrounded. The darkly lit room matches what he feels inside, an extreme amount of dark tension has gripped his heart. He has put off the meeting with Tom as long as he can, a decision must be made. His rank is shown by the brass and copper insignia on his collar and shoulder. These along with his glasses communicate an air of authority and experience.

Terence and Christian watch as Tom walks into the planning room. The meeting can finally start once Bill arrives. Terence wears a dark suit jacket and pants, a white undershirt and a black tie. He is not a soldier but still holds a lot of power within these meetings. Christian wears his dark gray captain uniform. He will be the second in command within this meeting today.

Bill finally arrives and looks at Tom as he enters the room. The group of the other three men stand by the bookcase and desk, watching as Bill keeps his eyes locked on Tom's. This meeting will end with their plan being finalized. Their soldiers have been surrounded and if they do not move soon, the war will be lost. Bill silences Tom with this look, communicating that now is not the time to speak.

Bill walks over to the three commanding officers and prepares to tell them what Tom already knows. If they do not act soon, all will be lost. They will take convincing but between Bill and Tom's information, they will understand that an all out offensive is their only hope for survival.
